Tulsi is considered the most sacred plant in Sanatan Dharma, directly associated with Lord Vishnu and believed to carry His divine presence continuously. The classic bead design is the traditional form preferred by most Vaishnavs for daily wear — simple, authentic, and deeply devotional. Wearing this mala maintains continuous connection with Vishnu's blessings, purifies the body and aura, and serves as a constant reminder of one's devotional path. Each mala is crafted from 100% genuine Tulsi wood, lab-certified using the Batch method, and energized by UPAASAK before dispatch. Suitable for devotees of all ages and all genders. Particularly recommended for new seekers of Vaishnav devotion looking for an authentic, traditionally crafted first Kanthi Mala. An essential and enduring spiritual accessory.

FAQs

What is the correct way to use a Jaap Mala?
Hold in the right hand with beads resting on the middle finger. Use the thumb to pull each bead while chanting. Never cross the Sumeru (Guru) bead — reverse direction instead.

Can I wear a Tulsi mala daily?
Yes — Tulsi Kanthi malas are for continuous daily wear. Jaap malas are for chanting practice and should be stored in a pouch when not in use.

Is Tulsi mala suitable for non-Vaishnavs?
Yes — while traditionally Vaishnav, Tulsi malas can be worn by anyone seeking health, peace, and spiritual protection.

Is it okay to wear a mala while sleeping?
It is traditionally recommended to remove the mala before sleeping and store it safely in a clean cotton pouch overnight.

Each mala is crafted from 100% genuine Tulsi wood, lab-certified using the Batch method, and energized by UPAASAK before dispatch. Suitable for devotees of all ages and highly recommended for daily japa practitioners. Store in a gomukhi (bead bag) when not in use for best results. What is the correct way to use a Jaap Mala?
Hold in the right hand with beads resting on the middle finger. Use the thumb to pull each bead while chanting. Never cross the Sumeru (Guru) bead — reverse direction instead.

Can I wear a Tulsi mala daily?
